title: Tech
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from Tech, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: Score, the dating app for people with good credit, is back
items.description: Two years ago, a controversial dating app was launched and quickly shuttered: for people with good-to-excellent credit. Now, the founder is relaunchin...

items.title: Cohere’s $240M year sets stage for IPO

items.title: Dutch phone giant Odido says millions of customers affected by data breach

items.title: Why top talent is walking away from OpenAI and xAI
items.description: AI companies have been hemorrhaging talent the past few weeks. Half of xAI’s founding team has left the company — some on their own, others through “r...

items.title: The SEC closed its investigation into Fisker
items.description: The financial regulator revealed in a response to a TechCrunch records request that the probe was closed in September 2025.

items.title: Amazon’s Ring cancels partnership with Flock, a network of AI cameras used by ICE, feds, and police
items.description: This news comes less than a week after Ring's Super Bowl commercial stoked controversy over the company's capacity for mass surveillance.

items.title: OpenAI removes access to sycophancy-prone GPT-4o model
items.description: The model is known for its overly sycophantic nature and its role in several lawsuits involving users' unhealthy relationships to the chatbot.

items.title: Fintech lending giant Figure confirms data breach

items.title: Meta plans to add facial recognition to its smart glasses, report claims
